title: Phlomoides iliensis
intro: Phlomoides iliensis, also known as Iliansk flaxflower, is a perennial plant species in the family Lamiaceae, native to Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Phlomoides iliensis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
